authority_boundary: These cases control route quality over live session-memory packets; raw, segment, receipt, and owner-local refs remain the stronger evidence.

review.note: Literal planner must stay structured-first for common route shapes, including exact session id rehydrate.

Search pressure packet must stay the fast route-first handoff that combines hotset, projection, rollup, direct-event, literal fallback, and live-scenario guidance without shard resampling, monolith reads, FTS, body hydration, or archive-truth claims.
Exact skill entity names must outrank shorter embedded MCP substrings when literal planner resolves a typed operational anchor.

literal_probes.name: mcp_transport_closed_noisy_human_phrase_keeps_entity_anchor
literal_probes.query: найди как aoa-session-memory-mcp возвращал Transport closed

review.note: A direct MCP Transport closed symptom must stay routed as the aoa-session-memory MCP entity first, then to transport preflight or stdio validation, not to broad raw text fallback.

literal_probes.name: path_inside_human_phrase_uses_path_anchor
literal_probes.query: как агент использовал validate_session_memory_mcp.py и что потом сломалось

review.note: Session labels embedded in natural-language answer queries must outrank accidental numeric/entity matches, and file-like paths embedded in human phrases must stay path anchors before raw text fallback.
Entity usage may warn on consequence depth, but it must not fail and must return direct raw or segment evidence.
The live consumer loop must prove it can sample several operational entity kinds, not only return evidence for one familiar anchor.
Graph bridge is useful only as a route companion with evidence refs; path_found is not required.
Tail operational entity classes must not depend on random spread: validator and test anchors must route through typed usage evidence with raw or segment refs.

Usage-chain must expose normalized action semantics over live operational entities so agents can distinguish invocation, inspection, validation, failure, and observed consequences without reading every event first.

Hook failure audit must prove the route can find recent failing UserPromptSubmit receipts and return receipt/session refs when failures exist; a healthy no-recent-errors window must stay explicit and non-actionable.
Goal lifecycle route must return actual lifecycle results with raw/segment evidence, not just an observed state summary.
Agent closeout audit must find completion/closeout answers with raw or segment refs so final answers can be audited separately from interim reasoning.
Agent-event routes must separately find answers, progress updates, final closeouts, and reasoning windows with raw/segment refs and raw-backed previews, without FTS or body hydration.
Task-answer chain route must find task intervals with user, reasoning-boundary, answer, and expansion refs directly from session indexes without requiring a goal lifecycle anchor.

Entity registry lookup must prove active, observed, unknown, stale, and removed status routing. Stale/removed are checked through a temporary previous-snapshot transition so the live archive is not mutated.
Graph neighborhood must stay a compact topology route with nodes, edges, and evidence refs; empty graph packets are route failures.

Dense operational anchors such as exec_command must use the bounded direct graph-store cooccurrence route, return neighboring route nodes and evidence refs, and expose truncation as route posture rather than an empty silent packet.

Route-rollup query must stay the fast consumer route over the materialized projection: human anchors such as aoa-session-memory-mcp must canonicalize to route keys without maintenance rebuild, shard resampling, monolith read, FTS, or raw hydration; returned refs remain the evidence handoff.

Direct operational-event rollup query must stay a fast materialized read-model for broad event classes such as result events; it must not resample shards, open the monolith, use FTS, hydrate bodies, or replace usage-chain/raw refs for behavior proof.
Direct operational-event rollup must support exact agent-event routes such as final closeouts without shard resampling, monolith reads, FTS, or body hydration.
Decision-lane rollup coverage must come from the typed decision_thread layer with raw or segment refs, not from incidental entity/path text that happens to contain decision.

Core operational route-rollup coverage must prove the recurring agent-work lanes through a materialized projection matrix, not a single convenient anchor. The packet remains navigation only; raw, segment, and session refs stay the evidence handoff.
Maintenance status must stay a read-only route packet that exposes typed next actions and an exact next command; corpus checks route guidance, not repair success.

Dense registered-entity graph edges may only be treated as replaceable after a live proof matches usage-chain event refs to event edges or reviewed post-prune aggregate refs and keeps the prune gate closed.

High-fanout replacement readiness may only close the before/after cardinality gate after a current read-only projection comparison proves lower cardinality or verified post-prune replacement coverage for every configured replacement route while keeping graph-row pruning closed.
Graph timeline must expose a bounded ordered goal-event chain with raw or segment refs and an explicit freshness status before structural event-chain fanout can be treated as replaceable.
